title: hr specialist path placement
description: A short quiz on the core ideas of the HR Specialist path. Answer what you know. Each chapter you place out of is one you can skip ahead on, and you can revisit any chapter any time.

scoring.chapterSkipRule: Each chapter is tested by 2 questions worth 2 points each. A learner places out of a chapter (skips its Version A) by answering both of its questions correctly. Overall, 50 percent or more of maxScore recommends Version B as the default for chapters not individually skipped.

questions.prompt: You want a tool that drafts a first-pass interview guide whenever a new role opens, with no one opening a chat window. What makes that possible?

options.label: Calling an AI model through its API from code, so the tool runs the model itself
Nothing, HR tasks cannot be automated
A shared folder of old documents
A longer job posting

questions.prompt: An AI model drafts a candidate summary. Because HR decisions affect people's livelihoods, what should you assume?

options.label: The draft can be wrong or biased, so a person reviews it and a person makes the decision
The model is neutral and correct, so it can decide on its own
AI output cannot be reviewed
Whatever it drafts is final

questions.prompt: You want AI help screening resumes against a specific role rubric. What gets you a consistent result?

options.label: Put the rubric and standards into the prompt as clear, explicit instructions
Assume the model knows your company's specific rubric
Give no instructions and accept whatever it returns
Use a shorter prompt every time

questions.prompt: An AI-drafted job description came back generic and missed required sections. What is the best fix?

options.label: Add the required sections, tone, and an example to the prompt, then run it again
Run the same prompt again unchanged
Give up on AI for job descriptions
Make the prompt vaguer
A packaged set of instructions, your playbook for a task, that Claude loads automatically when that task comes up
A faster version of the AI model
A single chat message you send once
A type of employee

questions.prompt: Before you deploy a new skill to your whole HR team, what should happen?

options.label: Check where it came from, evaluate it on real example cases, and name an owner for it
Push it to everyone immediately, skills are always safe
Keep it secret and let people find it by accident
Nothing, a skill needs no review

questions.prompt: An automation you are setting up needs an API key and will handle confidential employee records. How do you treat that key and data?

options.label: Keep secrets and sensitive data out of shared code and out of public places, and limit who can see them
Paste the API key directly into the code and share it widely so it is convenient
Post the key in a team chat so nobody loses it
Secrets do not need protecting

questions.prompt: Why should an API key never be written directly into code that gets shared or published?

options.label: Anyone who sees the code then has the key, and can use it as if they were you
It makes the code run slower
Keys stop working once they are written down
There is no reason, hardcoding keys is fine
